methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
MatX is a Series B chip design company founded by Reiner Pope, having raised $130M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 6 private peers in Chips & Semiconductors at the same or adjacent stage (Etched, d-Matrix, Positron AI, Substrate, DEEPX, and others), plus 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, using the $130M raised as a proxy baseline, yielding $247M. The Peer-Median approach anchors to the median valuation of $1.0B across the 6-company private comparable set, which ranges from $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple is not applicable; while public semiconductors trade at a 24.2x median multiple, MatX's revenue is not disclosed and cannot support this methodology. 

The triangulated valuation range is $210M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $624M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty about MatX's competitive position relative to better-capitalized peers like Etched ($5.0B). Stage-progression and peer-median methods diverge substantially, with the former suggesting earlier-stage pricing and the latter reflecting sector enthusiasm. Revenue visibility and technical differentiation against the peer set remain critical to narrowing this range.
